The paper search wasn't useful (returned unrelated ML papers). I'll rely on established amyloid kinetics knowledge (Knowles/Meisl/Cohen master equation), which I know well.

**Key analysis:**
- The NMSE is already excellent (~0.003). The bottleneck is **shape loss (~0.17)** — specifically the slope profile and response-crossing timing on delayed inhibitor curves.
- The parent (0.9569) has NO time forcing — its sigmoid shape comes purely from `(plateau - c)·(autocatalytic in c)`. This is autonomous, so it can only shift lag via the state trajectory, not enforce a distinct inflection timing per dose.
- Program 3 (best, 0.9582) added an explicit **maturation time-gate** `t/(tau·(1+K·cd)+t)` on the autocatalytic channels. This directly moves crossing times → lower shape loss.

The parent's structure is clean and compact. The most justified structural change: **add an explicit inhibitor-dependent time-gate on the autocatalytic (secondary) channel**, which is exactly what distinguishes the b

he redundant `smooth_state²` term, dropping from 9 to 8 constants. This keeps the fit well-conditioned and the expression compact (addressing the "solution length exceeds 500 chars" note).
- **Physical soundness:** Inhibition acts on the surface-catalysed secondary-nucleation channel (established Abeta42 mechanism) via both a bounded Langmuir rate gate and a time gate, both unity at cd=0. Mass conservation via `(plateau − c)`. All operations are smooth and singularity-free over the input ranges.
